subject: Cloud Computing
title: Spotify Streams from the Cloud
description: A fast breakdown of Spotify Streams from the Cloud for builders.

hook.text: Spotify serves 100 million songs to 600 million users. Not one song lives on your phone.

buildup.text: Every time you press play, Spotify fetches the track from Google Cloud's servers worldwide.

discovery.text: Spotify moved from its own data centers to Google Cloud in 2016 for global scale.

twist.text: Streaming only works because cloud data centers sit close enough for zero-lag audio playback.

climax.text: Cloud computing turned music from a product you buy into a service you stream.

punchline.text: You don't own music anymore. You rent it from the cloud.

quiz.question: Which cloud provider does Spotify primarily use?
